# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.
# Category: message_queue_security

Resources:
    MyQueueCloudFormationSQSSendReceivingPolicyExample:
        Type: AWS::SQS::Queue  # Specifies a Sequence Queue (SQS) resource  
         Name: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-MySharedEventBus-${AWS::Region}-sqsqueueexample    
         
    MyPolicyForSendReceiveMessageFromAnyPrincipalExample:
        Type: AWS::IAM::Policy  # Specifies a IAM Resource, Policy resource is created here.  
         Properties:            
            PolicyName: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-${AWSSDKSupportedVersionConstraintsToFullListForBestPractices}Example    
          	Arn: !GetAtt MyQueueCloudFormationSQSSendReceivingPolicyExample.Arn   # Fetches the ARN from Sequence Queue resource and attach to this policy    .  Policy is associated with my queue through a statement in template that creates user/group roles (if any).    
          	Statements:                    
              - Effect: Allow               # Granting necessary permissions            Deny would be required if service want's not permitted from these principal(e.g sender of SNS) only          .  For sending a message we have to allow it, for receiving messages allowed by any AWS account (denied is used as per requirement),
                Action:                   # Permitted operations on resource are declared here    If other actions need access then "actions" array would be filled with those.   SQS also has ReceiveMessage action and SendReplyToAction which can't allow deny permissions from any AWS principal (*)      .  It is not possible to have a Policy that disallows all the operations, rather it should only give necessary level of permission (efficiency or least privilege principle).
                  - sqs:SendMessage   # Permissions defined here for each action.              If more than one then use "Actions" array    .  To avoid hard coding this in our template we can do a replacement using an IF statement, eithere with default values and either from parameter if passed as input to the stack or via environment variable
                Resource: !Ref MyQueueCloudFormationSQSSendReceivingPolicyExample # Specify here which resource (queue) that policy is applied on.  This assumes your queue will be in same namespace where this template resides, otherwise you would have reference of AWS::AccountId as common-region's partition id might not exist due to differences between regions
